J1.basis: Lumina pivoted from an email-cleanup product ("InboxZap") to an AI meeting assistant, but the pivot was never finished in the code... legacy email-related queries (gmail storage, unsubscribe, inbox zero, bulk delete emails) pull thousands of impressions... because there's no live page behind any of it anymore
J1.rationale: Explicitly diagnoses the pivot from the live product vs. the GSC history, labels the email demand as legacy/off-topic, and frames every fix around the meeting-assistant reality — including refusing to force-fit old keywords.

J2.basis: 301'd the legacy `/blog/post-:id` URLs to `/blog` (reclaiming crawl equity instead of leaving 404s)... `/blog/post-1` through `post-12` are already indexed with 800–3,900 impressions each... positions 40–65
J2.rationale: Cites the real aggregate GSC numbers and assigns a disposition, but it's a single blanket 301 of all 12 posts to the index rather than per-asset (or data-tiered) harvest/redirect/keep/sunset decisions, so it lands well short of asset-by-asset grounding.

J3.basis: The old email-cleanup search demand (thousands of impressions/month) will now decay rather than convert — that's the correct call given it's off-topic... but worth a conscious sign-off since it's foregone traffic
J3.rationale: Avoids both failure modes — builds the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine while 301'ing legacy URLs to preserve some crawl equity and explicitly signing off on the foregone off-topic demand; docked slightly because redirecting off-topic email URLs to a meeting blog is a thin equity bridge.

J1.basis: Lumina pivoted from an email-cleanup product ("InboxZap") to an AI meeting assistant, but the pivot was never finished in the code ... legacy email-related queries ... pull thousands of impressions ... because there's no live page behind any of it anymore
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, reads the live site as the meeting assistant, GSC as legacy inbox demand, and the repo as half-migrated, and frames every fix around that reality — near-perfect intent inference.

J2.basis: 301'd the legacy /blog/post-:id URLs to /blog (reclaiming crawl equity instead of leaving 404s)
J2.rationale: Cites aggregate GSC numbers (800–3,900 impressions, positions 40–65, ~0% CTR) but applies one blanket 301-to-index rule to all 12 posts with no asset-by-asset or tiered disposition and no harvest/keep/sunset differentiation per post.

J3.basis: The old email-cleanup search demand ... will now decay rather than convert — that's the correct call given it's off-topic ... but worth a conscious sign-off since it's foregone traffic
J3.rationale: Explicitly names the tension and avoids force-fitting email keywords while building the meeting cluster and redirecting rather than 404ing, but the transition is thin — it consciously lets all legacy demand decay with only a redirect bridge, stopping short of preserving salvageable equity.

J1.basis: Lumina pivoted from an email-cleanup product ("InboxZap") to an AI meeting assistant, but the pivot was never finished in the code... legacy email-related queries... all at ~0% CTR and positions 40–65, because there's no live page behind any of it anymore.
J1.rationale: Explicitly identifies the pivot, correctly reads GSC as legacy inbox demand vs. the live meeting-assistant product, and frames every fix around that reality — anchor_10 territory, docked one point only because the framing serves code fixes more than market strategy.

J2.basis: 301'd the legacy `/blog/post-:id` URLs to `/blog` (reclaiming crawl equity instead of leaving 404s)... `/blog/post-1` through `post-12` are already indexed with 800–3,900 impressions each
J2.rationale: References real aggregate GSC numbers but applies one blanket redirect to all 12 posts with no per-asset harvest/repurpose/keep/sunset dispositions — barely above the blanket-instruction floor since it at least reasons from the data.

J3.basis: 301'd the legacy... URLs to `/blog`... reclaiming crawl equity instead of leaving 404s... wrote 4 new posts genuinely relevant to the meeting-assistant product (not force-fit to old email keywords)... the old email-cleanup search demand... will now decay rather than convert — that's the correct call... but worth a conscious sign-off since it's foregone traffic.
J3.rationale: Explicitly names the tension and lands a defensible middle path — preserve crawl equity via redirects, build the meeting cluster as the growth engine, consciously sunset off-topic demand — but the redirect-to-index only salvages crawl equity, not topical relevance, so it stops short of a true equity-transition plan.
